primary.justification: The chapter's entire architecture is organised around explaining the formation of the Indian Constitution — the process (Constituent Assembly, Drafting Committee, 1946–1950), the ideals of the freedom movement enshrined in it, civilisational heritage embedded in it, influences from other countries' constitutions, key features, and the Preamble's guiding values. Remove this competency and every named section collapses. The chapter is the canonical treatment of C-8.2's demands: process of formation, ideals of the national movement, and India's civilisational heritage as sources. Political Science sub-discipline.

Two architecturally distinct sections develop C-8.1's demands: (1) 'Why Do We Need a Constitution?' is a dedicated section explaining the deeper objectives and necessity of a constitution for governing a large, diverse, complex country — directly meeting C-8.1's demand to understand the need for a constitution; (2) 'Understanding the Preamble' is a dedicated section systematically analysing each constitutional value (Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic, Republic, Justice, Liberty, Equality, Fraternity) as deeper objectives of the Indian Constitution. Both sections could stand as independent learning units. Rule 5(a) satisfied.
